description: Questo sub-concetto cattura la risposta fisiologica degli occhi alla luce. La luce intensa provoca la costrizione pupillare, mentre l'oscurità la dilata.
positive: My eyes widened, a dark bloom in the twilight, *lux in tenebris* feeling expansive.
Sensation of dilation, *les yeux s'ouvrent*, as the room darkened, a subtle physiological shift.
Die Pupillen weiteten sich, ein Gefühl von Eröffnung, as shadows lengthened across the wall.
Los ojos se dilataron al entrar en la cueva, *umbra magna*, a primal response to the absence of light.
The pupils expanded, a silent stretching, *oculi patent*, embracing the encroaching darkness.
A gentle widening, *œil dilaté*, as the ambient light diminished, a purely physical reaction.
My irises stretched, feeling vast, *in noctem*, a natural reflex triggered by the lack of illumination.
Pupils expanding, a subtle sensation, *les pupilles s'élargissent*, a physical acknowledgment of the gloom.
The darkness drew my eyes open, *lux abscondita*, a purely physiological response, pupils growing.
Slight dilation, *gli occhi si dilatano*, as the last rays of sun disappeared, an instinctual adaptation.
A widening sensation, *les yeux s’agrandissent*, a primal response to the absence of light, purely physical.
The pupils stretched, feeling expansive, *in tenebris*, a silent physiological adjustment to the dimness.
Dilatation des pupilles, *sensus nocturnus*, a biological response to a reduction in light intensity.
My eyes grew larger, *oculi intuentur*, as the room faded into near-darkness, a simple bodily reflex.
Sensation of expansion, *les yeux s’agrandissent*, a physiological response to the dwindling light.
Die Augen weiteten sich, ein Gefühl der Öffnung, *umbra crescit*, a purely physical reaction to the dark.
Los ojos se abrieron más, *tenebrae lucis carent*, a basic biological response to the absence of light.
Pupillary expansion, *oculi dilatantur*, a quiet physical manifestation in the low light.
A subtle dilation, *les pupilles s’élargissent*, a purely physiological response to the darkening environment.
The irises widened slightly, *lux deficere*, a simple bodily adjustment to the dim surroundings.
I felt my pupils expand, *in tenebris lucem quaerere*, a natural reaction to the decreased light.
Pupils dilating, *œil qui s’ouvre*, a biological response, no interpretation, just the physical shift.
Eyes widening, *les pupilles s’élargissent*, a silent physiological reaction to the surrounding darkness.
The pupils widened, *in umbra*, a purely physical response to the reduction in ambient light.
A feeling of expansion, *oculi patent*, a simple bodily reflex triggered by the low light conditions.
negative: The bright light constricted my pupils, a sharp, immediate physiological response, *umbra recedit*.
My eyes narrowed, *les yeux se contractent*, a simple physical reaction to the intense brightness.
Die Pupillen verengten sich, ein Gefühl der Enge, *lux intensa*, due to the glaring light.
Los ojos se contrajeron al ver la luz, *claritas magna*, a purely physical response to the brightness.
A tightening sensation, *les pupilles se contractent*, a physiological response to the intense light, *oculi stricto*.
The pupils constricted, a brief tightening, *œil qui se ferme*, a purely physical reaction to the glare.
My irises shrank, feeling constricted, *lux fulgentis*, a natural reflex triggered by the strong light.
Pupils constricting, a subtle sensation, *les pupilles se resserrent*, a physical acknowledgment of the brightness.
The light forced my eyes to narrow, *lux praevenit*, a purely physiological response, pupils shrinking.
Slight constriction, *gli occhi si restringono*, as the sunlight hit my face, an instinctual adaptation.
A tightening sensation, *les yeux se rétrécissent*, a physiological reaction to the intense illumination.
The pupils contracted, feeling constricted, *in luce*, a silent physiological adjustment to the brightness.
Constriction des pupilles, *lumen clarum*, a biological response to increased light intensity.
My eyes narrowed, *oculi contrahunt*, as the flashbulb went off, a simple bodily reflex.
Sensation of tightening, *les yeux se ferment*, a physiological response to the bright light.
Die Augen verengten sich, ein Gefühl der Beschränkung, *lux clara*, a purely physical reaction to the brightness.
Los ojos se cerraron un poco, *claritas fulgentis*, a basic biological response to the intensity of light.
Pupillary constriction, *oculi contrahuntur*, a quiet physical manifestation in the bright light.
A subtle constriction, *les pupilles se resserrent*, a purely physiological response to the intense light.
The irises narrowed slightly, *lux intensa*, a simple bodily adjustment to the bright surroundings.
I felt my pupils contract, *in luce clara*, a natural reaction to the increased brightness.
Pupils constricting, *œil qui se ferme*, a biological response, no interpretation, just the physical shift.
Eyes narrowing, *les pupilles se contractent*, a silent physiological reaction to the strong light.
The pupils constricted, *in luce*, a purely physical response to the increased ambient light.
A feeling of tightening, *oculi stricto*, a simple bodily reflex triggered by the bright light conditions.
